body.u: Looking back on my life I see nothing particularly exciting or eventful in the past.

I left school at eighteen with, I thought adequate qualifications that would net me a ‘satisfactory’ job. However all the jobs I did have, inevitableinevitably involved clerical work of one description or another, which meant, though boredom mainly, that I didn’t hold them down long. Perhaps eventually it was my fault that I got landed with those sorts of jobs, after all I did decide to work at school, which when I look back now was fortunate, for a manual job would have horrible, compared to a typically clerical job, and those were at least tolerable.

Money was whole point of this ‘the Rat Race’ which started at school and hasn’t yet finished. The whole idea you see, was that by working at school and gaining qualifications, you didn’t have to work so much when it came to a job, be nice if things were like that.

As for as making money is concerned I was surprised acidemicacademic qualifications were in many cases. 1 an old friend of mine when I met in my second job made his fortune by being a burgalerburglar. Obviously in that occupation, academic qualifications were unnessecararyunnecessary which was just as well for him since he had none. In the process of his criminal activities he made use of a network of friends all of whom he could trust, and that’s why he never caught.

One particular thing I’ve learnt is that money is important, no matter what people say. True, just because you have a lot of money doesn’t mean to say you’ll be happy, but it helps. Money provides power, a useful thing to have.

If you ever have to chosechoose between a friend say, and the state, when it comes to a clash of loyalties always chosechoose the friend, because to the state, be you servant, informer or what and how {irrelevant?} ever, you are still a statistic, a mark on a piece of paper to be stored, accounted for and classified to a friend you are a person.
As for advice what you do, do your self and don’t accept anything the government says, unless it matches your opinion.
